skill.name: background-remover
skill.description: Remove backgrounds from JPG, PNG, and WebP images through the verging.ai API, with support for local files and remote image URLs.
skill.summary: Guides AI agents through verging.ai background removal jobs and result downloads.
skill.author: verging.ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, environment, filesystem, and weak-cryptography patterns. Review confirms legitimate third-party API behavior for image processing, but the skill sends API keys and images to external services and returns public result URLs. No prompt injection, bundled malware, or confirmed malicious intent was found; weak-cryptography alerts are false positives from Markdown text.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Image Upload and Public Result URL
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to upload user images to verging.ai and R2 storage, then return processed images through a public URL. This is legitimate for the advertised service, but it creates privacy and data exposure risk for sensitive images.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented processing flow explicitly uploads images and stores results behind public URLs. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the risk is privacy exposure rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: API Key Used in Credentialed Network Requests
medium_findings.description: The skill requires VERGING_API_KEY or an explicit API key and sends it in Authorization headers for curl requests. This is expected for the service, but copied commands or command-line flags can expose secrets through logs, shell history, or process listings.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key requirement and Authorization header examples are explicit. The endpoint is the advertised provider, so this is an operational secret-handling concern rather than evidence of exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: User-Provided Remote URLs May Be Fetched
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents to download remote image URLs with curl before processing. Without validation guidance, this could fetch unintended internal, private, or untrusted resources from the execution environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The remote URL workflow is clearly documented, but no implementation is bundled. The risk depends on how the agent validates URLs before running curl.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Mostly Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick detections map to fenced command examples and Markdown instructions, not bundled executable code. The skill still asks agents to use curl, so command execution should be user-visible and limited to the documented workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown and contains no separate script file. Some command execution remains intentional because the instructions tell the agent to call curl.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography locations contain descriptive Markdown, tables, or skill metadata. No evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic implementation was found in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain no cryptographic code or algorithm selection. This is a strong false positive from keyword matching in prose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credentialed curl Requests to External API
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents curl calls that send VERGING_API_KEY to verging.ai endpoints in Authorization headers. This pattern is necessary for the provider workflow but requires careful secret handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit in multiple curl examples. The destination is the advertised service, which lowers malicious confidence but preserves operational risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Upload to Presigned Storage and Public Image URLs
dangerous_patterns.description: The processing flow uploads image files to presigned storage and returns result images through public URLs. Users should treat uploaded images as shared with a third-party processor.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The storage and public URL behavior is directly documented. It is expected for the skill but important for marketplace disclosure.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Image URL Download Through curl
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill allows remote image URLs and instructs agents to download them locally before upload. URL validation and user confirmation should be used for unknown or private links.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The remote URL path is clear, but the exact execution behavior depends on the host agent. The concern is credible but not evidence of malicious code.

content.value_statement: Product photos and portraits often need clean transparent backgrounds. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through verging.ai image upload, job creation, and result download.
actual_capabilities: Accepts local or remote JPG, PNG, and WebP image inputs.
Uses VERGING_API_KEY or an explicit API key for verging.ai authentication.
Checks account information and credits before starting a job.
Uploads image files to presigned storage for background removal.
Creates and polls background removal jobs until completion.
Returns a result URL and can download the processed image locally.
limitations: Requires a verging.ai account, API key, and available credits.
Uploads images to an external service for processing.
Supports only JPG, PNG, and WebP files up to 10 MB.
Does not provide offline or local background removal.

use_cases.title: Prepare Product Photos
use_cases.description: Remove backgrounds from catalog images so products can appear on clean storefront layouts.

use_cases.title: Create Profile Headshots
use_cases.description: Turn portrait photos into transparent-background images for resumes, bios, and profile pages.

use_cases.title: Build Design Assets
use_cases.description: Create transparent cutouts for presentations, social posts, and marketing graphics.

prompt_templates.title: Remove a Local Background
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the background from ./photo.jpg and return the transparent image URL.

prompt_templates.title: Process a Remote Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Use an image URL as the source
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the background remover on this public image URL and show the processing status until it finishes.

prompt_templates.title: Save the Result Locally
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the background from ./product.png, download the result, and save it to ./output/product-transparent.png.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Credits Before Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my verging.ai credits, confirm the image format and size, then remove the background from ./portrait.webp.

output_examples.input: A local product photo named shoe.jpg
output_examples.output: The skill reports that the job completed and provides a transparent-background product image URL.
The skill downloads the image, submits it for processing, shows progress, and returns the finished result link.

output_examples.input: A local PNG with a requested output path
output_examples.output: The skill completes background removal and saves the transparent PNG to the requested local path.

best_practices: Store the API key in VERGING_API_KEY instead of pasting it into prompts.
Use a dedicated API key with the minimum permissions needed.
Confirm that images can be shared with verging.ai before upload.
anti_patterns: Do not upload confidential, regulated, or private images without approval.
Do not paste API keys into shared chat logs or public files.
Do not process unknown remote URLs before checking the source.

faq.question: Does this skill run background removal locally?
faq.answer: No. It sends images to the verging.ai API for processing.

faq.question: Which image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill documents support for JPG, PNG, and WebP files up to 10 MB.

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill requires a verging.ai API key, usually through VERGING_API_KEY.

faq.question: Can it use image URLs?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports remote image URLs and downloads them before processing.

faq.question: Where are processed images returned?
faq.answer: The skill returns a result URL and can download the image when requested.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive images?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflow uploads images to an external service and may return public result URLs.
